title: High Throughput Processing of Wastewater for SARS-CoV-2 RNA Analysis
author: Aaron Topol
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.b2kmqcu6

steps.title: Process fresh samples stored at 4 °C if possible
steps.instruction: Process fresh samples stored at 4 °C if possible. If analyzing a frozen sample, thaw at Room temperature . Set centrifuge temperature to 4 °C . Set oven temperature to 110 °C . Remove an aliquot of BCoV from the freezer and thaw On ice .

steps.title: Label 50 mL conicals with the sample name
steps.instruction: Label 50 mL conicals with the sample name.

steps.title: Invert the sludge sample bottle a few times to mix
steps.instruction: Invert the sludge sample bottle a few times to mix.

steps.title: In a biosafety cabinet, pour 50...
steps.instruction: In a biosafety cabinet, pour 50 mL of well mixed primary sludge sample to corresponding labeled 50 mL conical. If the total volume of a sample is less than 50 mL, bring the volume up to 50 mL with nuclease free water.

steps.title: Bring the rotor to a biosafety...
steps.instruction: Bring the rotor to a biosafety cabinet and arrange the conicals in the rotor in a manner to ensure that it is balanced.

steps.title: Centrifuge samples at 24000 x g,...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge samples at 24000 x g, °C, for at least 00:30:00 at 4 °C . Set the Centrifuge time to “Hold”.

steps.title: In a biosafety cabinet, aspirate supernatant...
steps.instruction: In a biosafety cabinet, aspirate supernatant into a liquid biohazard waste container.

steps.title: Apply a label to a new...
steps.instruction: Apply a label to a new 50 mL conical for each sample and record the empty conical mass in grams.

steps.title: With a spatula (either plastic or...
steps.instruction: With a spatula (either plastic or metal), scoop a small aliquot of dewatered pellet (~0.75 g) into the new, pre-weighed 50 mL conical. Record the mass in grams of the aliquot in the conical. Set the rest of the pellet aside for dry weight measurement. It is important to note the wet mass of solids in this conical as it is essential in resuspending the solids in buffer to the right concentration and back calculating the concentration of the gene targets per dry mass of solids.

steps.title: In a new 150 mL bottle,...
steps.instruction: In a new 150 mL bottle, prepare a stock solution of DNA/RNA shield with BCoV spike-in by adding 1.5 µL rehydrated BCoV vaccine per 1 mL DNA/RNA shield. Mix well by inverting or gently shaking the bottle. The concentration of BCoV target is approximately 500,000 copies / ml in this solution. DNA/RNA Shield Zymo Research Catalog #R1100-50 Bovine coronavirus Zoetis Catalog #CALF-GUARD

steps.title: Add DNA/RNA shield with BCoV spike-in...
steps.instruction: Add DNA/RNA shield with BCoV spike-in to each conical containing solids so that the final concentration of dewatered solids resuspended in the BCoV-spiked DNA/RNA shield is ~ 75 mg dewatered solids per ml BCoV-spiked DNA/RNA shield was chosen during an exercise where we titrated the solids in the mixture (spiked with gRNA of SARS-CoV-2) to determine the maximum mass per volume where we did not see inhibition of the SARS-CoV-2 assays in digital PCR. You may want to repeat this titration with your samples to ensure it is appropriate for alleviating inhibition. 75 mg/mL 75 mg dewatered solids per ml BCoV-spiked DNA/RNA shield was chosen during an exercise where we titrated the solids in the mixture (spiked with gRNA of SARS-CoV-2) to determine the maximum mass per volume where we did not see inhibition of the SARS-CoV-2 assays in digital PCR. You may want to repeat this titration with your samples to ensure it is appropriate for alleviating inhibition.

steps.title: Save the remaining DNA/RNA shield with...
steps.instruction: Save the remaining DNA/RNA shield with BCoV spike-in to use as template in Extraction Positive Control during RNA Extraction.

steps.title: Add 5-10 grinding balls to each conical tube
steps.instruction: Add 5-10 grinding balls to each conical tube.

steps.title: Place the conicals in the adapters...
steps.instruction: Place the conicals in the adapters on the stage of the Geno/Grinder and tightly secure the clamp. Geno/Grinder (Automated Tissue Homogenizer and Cell Lyser, SPEX Sample Prep, 1006-113806)

steps.title: Shake the conicals in the Geno/Grinder...
steps.instruction: Shake the conicals in the Geno/Grinder for 00:02:00 at 1,000 rpm to homogenize the sample.

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge the conicals for 00:05:00...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge the conicals for 00:05:00 at 5250 x g, °C, to pull down the bubbles generated during homogenization. Sample is ready for RNA extraction using the Chemagic 360 (see accompanying protocol, extraction may also be done using Kingfisher or several manual Qiagen kits. See protocol for details) . A modification of this step may provide greater sensitivity. The modification entails centrifuging at a higher speed of 24000 x g, °C, to pellet the solids and then extracting the RNA from the resultant supernatant.

steps.title: Place aluminum weigh boats in oven...
steps.instruction: Place aluminum weigh boats in oven to pre-heat to 110 °C .

steps.title: Weigh each pan and record the...
steps.instruction: Weigh each pan and record the mass in grams of each empty pan.

steps.title: Using a spatula, scoop a generous...
steps.instruction: Using a spatula, scoop a generous amount of dewatered solid into the pan.

steps.title: Record the mass in grams of...
steps.instruction: Record the mass in grams of each pan with the wet solids.

steps.title: Place the dishes in the oven...
steps.instruction: Place the dishes in the oven inside the biosafety cabinet and leave overnight to dry,

steps.title: The next day or at least...
steps.instruction: The next day or at least 6 hours later ( 06:00:00 - 24:00:00 dry time), remove the pans from the oven and record the mass in grams. The dry weight percent is calculated as: (100)*(1 - (wet mass of solids - dry mass of solids) / (wet mass of solids)) This percentage is key for calculation of the concentration of the gene targets per mass dry weight.

steps.title: Dispose of pans in the red biohazard waste bin
steps.instruction: Dispose of pans in the red biohazard waste bin.

steps.title: Apply a label for each sample...
steps.instruction: Apply a label for each sample to a 50 mL conical.

steps.title: Pour up to 50 mL of...
steps.instruction: Pour up to 50 mL of remaining primary settled solids sample and place at 4 °C for storage until it is confirmed that results of the analyses pass quality control. Dewatered solids can be stored at -80 deg C. We recommend that you confirm the effects of storage on target quantification. We have noted that up to 7 d storage at 4 deg C has minimal affect on the RNA targets. However, freeze thaws may affect target quantification.

steps.title: Dispose of remaining sludge in 4...
steps.instruction: Dispose of remaining sludge in 4 L Biohazard waste container prefilled with 400 mL of bleach. Then dispose of the sample bottles as biohazard waste.
